title: Authentication Service Outage - DB Connection Pool Exhaustion
root_cause: Database connection pool exhausted due to connection leak in auth-service v2.3.1. A code change introduced in the previous deployment failed to close DB connections in the error handling path of the /validate-token endpoint. When token validation errors occurred, the connection was not returned to the pool, causing gradual exhaustion over ~30 minutes.
resolution: Rolled back auth-service to v2.3.0. Implemented connection pool monitoring with Prometheus. Added connection leak detection test in CI/CD pipeline. Patched the error handler to use try/finally for connection release.

timeline.event: auth-service v2.3.1 deployed to production (4 replicas)
Connection pool utilization begins climbing above normal baseline
First ERROR: connection pool exhausted, requests failing
api-gateway circuit breaker opens for auth-service
On-call engineer initiates rollback to v2.3.0

lessons_learned: Add connection pool metrics to Grafana dashboards (pool utilization, wait time, leaked connections)
Implement connection leak detection tests in CI/CD pipeline
Set up alerts for pool utilization > 80%
All database code must use try/finally or context managers for connection release
Add synthetic load test that simulates error conditions to catch leak patterns
